stem: A program stores the test scores of a class of 30 pupils using a one-dimensional array called scores.
model_answer: A one-dimensional array is a fixed-size collection of elements of the same data type stored under one identifier. Each element is accessed using an index (position), starting at 0 in most languages. In this case scores[0] would be the first pupil's score and scores[29] the last. Arrays make it easy to process many related values with a loop, for example to find the highest score.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe how a one-dimensional array stores the pupils' scores and how an individual value is accessed.

mark_points.text: array holds multiple values of the same data type under one identifier
accepted_alternatives: one name stores many items of the same type

mark_points.text: each element has a unique index/position (often starting at 0)
an individual score is accessed by its index, e.g. scores[3] for the 4th pupil
accepted_alternatives: use array[index] to read/write one value
